title: Grafana Dashboard Version History and Revert Event Tracking
exclusions: Does not replace dedicated datasource query auditing, image renderer health, LDAP group synchronization analytics, notification delivery monitoring, or generic API token abuse detections catalogued under sibling Grafana use cases.
evidence: Saved search grafana_uc_13_6_7_dashboard_version_lineage with scheduled alert exports and weekly CSV archives to a restricted observability governance index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In staging, an operator saves a dashboard, triggers a version restore, and emits matching grafana:audit and grafana:webhook rows with the same dashboard_uid within one minute, producing a CRITICAL or HIGH row when the board is flagged executive and scheduled_window is empty.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During a documented maintenance window with scheduled_window containing approved maintenance text, the same restore action produces only LOW or MEDIUM severity rows and no pager when policy expects suppression.

dataSources: index=observability sourcetype=grafana:audit for native Grafana audit events covering dashboard save, update, delete, restore, version, revert, and rollback actions with dashboard_uid, org_id, and actor; index=observability sourcetype=grafana:webhook for Grafana Enterprise outbound webhook deliveries of dashboard saved and version restored events to Splunk HTTP Event Collector; Splunk CIM Change data model accelerated for Change.object_category=dashboard with Change.action modified or created extracted from audit or webhook pipelines; Kubernetes API audit or admission review logs for ConfigMap changes in namespaces that mount Grafana dashboard providers; index=cicd sourcetype=argocd:audit for Argo CD application sync and resource events touching dashboard ConfigMaps or provisioning paths; index=cicd sourcetype=flux:reconcile for Flux Kustomization or Helm reconciliation summaries referencing Grafana dashboard providers or sidecar reload annotations; optional index=observability sourcetype=k8s:audit when admission controllers validate grafana-dashboard ConfigMaps
app: Grafana Enterprise with auditing.enabled and optional Enterprise audit logging pipeline, or Grafana OSS with file-based grafana.log plus Splunk Universal Forwarder or Fluent Bit shipping to HTTP Event Collector; props.conf stanza typically [grafana:audit] with JSON or KV extraction
spl:
`comment("UC-13.6.7 Grafana dashboard version lineage: fuse audit, enterprise webhook, CIM Change dashboards, GitOps reconcile. Baseline window 30d; tune saves_per_hour thresholds; no draft tokens in production.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="grafana:audit" earliest=-30d@h latest=now
      (dashboard OR dashboard_uid OR uid OR "dashboard_uid")
      | eval act_lc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action, verb, operation, request_action, ""))))
      | eval raw_lc=lower(_raw)
      | where match(act_lc,"(save|saved|update|restore|restored|version|revert|rollback)") OR match(raw_lc,"(save|restore|version|revert|rollback).*dashboard|dashboard.*(save|restore|version|revert|rollback)")
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, uid, dashboardUid, dashboardUID, resource_uid, ""))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user, username, login, actor, user_email, "")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id, orgId, orgID, organization_id, "")))
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action, verb, operation, "audit_action"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_audit" ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="grafana:webhook" earliest=-30d@h latest=now
      (dashboard OR "dashboard-version" OR uid)
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, uid, dashboardUid, dashboardUID, ""))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user, username, editor, triggeredBy, "")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id, orgId, orgID, "")))
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(event_type, action, hookType, "webhook_event"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_webhook" ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) AS _time FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-30d@h latest=now (Change.action="modified" OR Change.action="created") Change.object_category="dashboard" BY Change.object Change.user Change.action
      | rename Change.object AS dashboard_uid Change.user AS user Change.action AS cim_action
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(dashboard_uid)))
      | eval user=trim(toString(user))
      | eval org_id="unknown"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(cim_action)))
      | eval lane="cim_change" ]
    [ search index=cicd (sourcetype="argocd:audit" OR sourcetype="flux:reconcile") earliest=-30d@h latest=now
      (grafana OR dashboard OR ConfigMap OR configmap OR provider)
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, uid, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?i)(?:uid|dashboard)[=:\"\s]+(?<rex_uid>[A-Za-z0-9_-]{6,64})"
      | eval dashboard_uid=if(len(dashboard_uid)>0, dashboard_uid, lower(trim(toString(mvindex(rex_uid,0)))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user, username, principal, triggeredBy, "gitops_controller")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id, cluster, "")))
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verb, operation, reason, "gitops_reconcile"))))
      | eval lane="gitops" ]
| eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, ""))))
| eval user=coalesce(nullif(trim(user),""), "unknown_actor")
| eval org_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(org_id),""), "unknown_org")
| where len(dashboard_uid) > 3
| eval action_normalized=lower(trim(toString(event_action)))
| sort 0 dashboard_uid + _time
| streamstats window=1 current=f global=f last(_time) AS prev_ts BY dashboard_uid
| eval secs_since_last_edit=if(isnotnull(prev_ts) AND prev_ts>0, round(_time - prev_ts, 0), null())
| streamstats time_window=3600s global=f count AS saves_last_hour BY user
| eventstats count AS edits_baseline_30d BY dashboard_uid
| eval baseline_edits_per_day=round(edits_baseline_30d / 30, 3)
| eval hr=tonumber(strftime(_time, "%H"), 10)
| eval after_hours=if((hr < 7 OR hr > 18), 1, 0)
| eval is_revert=if(match(action_normalized, "(revert|rollback|restore)"), 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 dashboard_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv
     | fields dashboard_uid, title, folder, owner, classification, is_executive, gitops_provider, scheduled_window
     | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(dashboard_uid))) ]
| eval is_exec=if(match(lower(toString(is_executive)), "^(1|true|yes)$"), 1, 0)
| eval sched_lc=lower(trim(toString(scheduled_window)))
| eval in_change_window=if(match(sched_lc, "(cab|approved|maintenance|green|saturday|sunday|sat|sun)"), 1, if(len(sched_lc)==0, 0, 1))
| eval severity=case(
    is_revert==1 AND is_exec==1 AND in_change_window==0, "CRITICAL",
    saves_last_hour > 5, "HIGH",
    after_hours==1 AND match(action_normalized, "(save|update|modified|created)"), "MEDIUM",
    true(), "LOW"
  )
| table _time, dashboard_uid, title, folder, user, org_id, lane, action_normalized, saves_last_hour, secs_since_last_edit, baseline_edits_per_day, severity, classification, is_executive, gitops_provider
description: Dashboard JSON inside Grafana is one of the most consequential change surfaces in modern observability: a saved revision moves executive KPIs, incident command layouts, and regulated readouts faster than many deployment pipelines. Version history, restore points, rollback clicks, and GitOps-driven reprovisions can interleave in ways that confuse reviewers unless every mutation lands in one tamper-resistant ledger. This use case stitches native audit lines, enterprise webhook deliveries, normalized change records for dashboard objects, and continuous delivery signals when ConfigMap or file providers refresh. It highlights edit wars between operators with overlapping folder scopes, automation loops that repeatedly stamp the same uid, mistaken rollbacks during drills, and silent divergence when UI saves fight provisioning reconcilers. Unlike UC-13.6.10 permission drift, UC-13.6.5 login auditing, UC-13.6.6 annotation storms, UC-13.6.13 notification failures, UC-13.6.8 API token usage patterns, UC-13.6.4 plugin skew, UC-13.6.11 identity sync mistakes, or repeated UC-13.6.13 notification emphasis, this narrative centers dashboard payload version events, GitOps versus interactive authorship, after-hours reverts, and executive dashboard governance.
value: Leaders and auditors gain a single timeline that proves who moved each operational picture, when a rollback happened, and whether automation or a human authored the change. That clarity shortens investigations after confusing incidents, preserves trustworthy executive views, and gives platform teams evidence to enforce dashboard-as-code without blind spots.
implementation: Forward Grafana audit JSON or enterprise webhooks into index=observability with sourcetype=grafana:audit and grafana:webhook, align GitOps audit feeds to index=cicd, accelerate CIM Change for dashboard objects, maintain grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv, schedule grafana_uc_13_6_7_dashboard_version_lineage with earliest=-30d@h, and route CRITICAL executive rollback rows to owners within fifteen minutes.
visualization: Time-series of version events per dashboard_uid, heatmap of saves_last_hour by user, table of CRITICAL reverts with lookup owner, and GitOps lane overlay for reconcile spikes.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-7d@h latest=now Change.object_category="dashboard" BY Change.object Change.user Change.action
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Change summaries with object_category=dashboard for at least thirty days so the tstats arm reflects save and modify activity without scanning raw indexes; rebuild if Grafana field extractions change.

references.title: Argo CD — Resource hooks and sync waves

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate dashboard-as-code refresh cadence from Flux or Argo CD reconciliation can emit bursts that resemble human edit wars even though only the GitOps bot touched the uid; require correlation with sourcetype=flux:reconcile or argocd:audit before paging operators. Kubernetes dashboard reloader controllers that watch ConfigMaps may bump dashboardVersion on every reload even when JSON is byte-identical to the prior state; pair version increments with checksums or diff tools to ignore no-op churn. Terraform Cloud or other remote-plan systems that provision templated dashboards through the HTTP API often create modified events without a human principal; classify those service accounts in the lookup to downgrade noise. On-call rotations sometimes restore a board during a handover using the Grafana UI; if scheduled_window documents that practice, severity should remain MEDIUM rather than CRITICAL. Incident management exercises frequently roll dashboards forward and back to prove runbooks; tag those windows in the lookup or suppress via change_ticket_id fields when present. Major Grafana upgrades may rewrite schema fields so dashboardVersion increments even though visual panels are unchanged; treat the first week after upgrade as a calibration period. Cosmetic edits such as panel resize or legend toggles can increment dashboardVersion without altering queries; baseline math must compare against historical edit frequency rather than assuming every bump is structural. Executive dashboards duplicated into staging orgs can mirror saves that look like production tampering unless org_id filtering is strict. Synthetic monitoring accounts that snapshot boards for rendering tests may save automatically; exclude their usernames via a secondary lookup. Provisioning disableDeletion=false with aggressive GitOps may recreate boards users deleted intentionally, appearing as unauthorized recreation until policy is clarified.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with observability platform engineers, Grafana administrators, identity and access reviewers, and the teams that operate GitOps controllers that provision dashboard providers. This use case treats Grafana dashboards as operational artefacts with the same seriousness as routing rules or deployment manifests: every saved revision, restored snapshot, version jump, revert, and rollback is a governance event that can move executive KPIs, regulated readouts, or incident command boards without a pull request. The narrative spans native audit streams, enterprise webhook deliveries, normalized Splunk CIM Change rows for dashboard objects, and continuous delivery audit lines when ConfigMap-backed or file-provider bundles refresh from Argo CD or Flux.

Scope differs from sibling Grafana observability use cases already catalogued. UC-13.6.10 focuses on folder and permission drift rather than payload version lineage. UC-13.6.5 covers interactive login and session establishment, not who rewrote which board. UC-13.6.6 tracks annotation storms and overlay churn, which can correlate with dashboard saves but does not reconstruct version history. UC-13.6.13 addresses notification channel failures rather than dashboard JSON mutations. UC-13.6.8 watches API token abuse across endpoints, not deliberate rollback of a specific uid. UC-13.6.4 highlights plugin and renderer skew, not content edits. UC-13.6.11 covers LDAP or OAuth team synchronization mistakes, which may change who can edit yet does not emit the save event itself. UC-13.6.13 notification failures are orthogonal to revert storms. This UC-13.6.7 thread is the payload-centric audit trail: saved versions, restore and rollback actions, GitOps reconciliation versus interactive UI saves, edit wars between operators with overlapping scopes, automation loops that stamp identical dashboards, and unauthorized mutations to executive or regulated layouts.

Risk posture: Grafana High Availability pairs and read replicas must share accurate clocks; otherwise streamstats ordering looks like impossible rapid reverts. Secret material for service accounts that call the dashboard versions API belongs in a vault-backed credential broker, not long-lived shell exports on analyst laptops. Legal and privacy reviewers should approve retention for dashboard titles and folder paths that may embed customer identifiers. Break-glass accounts must still emit audit rows; do not exclude security-admins from the feed.

Index layout: land Grafana audit JSON or key-value logs in index=observability with sourcetype=grafana:audit. Land Grafana Enterprise outbound webhook deliveries, including dashboard saved hooks, as sourcetype=grafana:webhook in the same index unless your routing standards mandate a dedicated webhook index. Mirror Kubernetes API audit or admission review logs that touch grafana-dashboards ConfigMaps into index=observability or index=cicd depending on your platform convention. Route Argo CD audit and Flux Kustomization or Helm reconciliation summaries that mention dashboard providers into index=cicd with sourcetype=argocd:audit or sourcetype=flux:reconcile. Maintain at least thirty days hot retention for baseline frequency math in this search.

Field expectations: dashboard_uid must align with the stable uid returned by the HTTP GET /api/dashboards/uid/:uid/versions endpoint. user should map to the Grafana username, email, or service account label present in audit records. org_id captures multi-tenant separation. action or verb fields should reveal save, update, restore, version, revert, or rollback semantics even when vendors rename them between releases.

Lookup grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv is curator-owned with columns dashboard_uid, title, folder, owner, classification, is_executive, gitops_provider, and scheduled_window. classification might read internal, regulated, or customer-facing. is_executive flags boards that feed leadership reviews. gitops_provider names argo, flux, terraform_cloud, or none. scheduled_window carries free-text change-window hints such as cab saturday or maintenance green that drive severity logic. Curators should refresh the CSV at least daily from the Grafana HTTP API inventory export so join coverage stays aligned with live folders.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Enable Grafana auditing in grafana.ini with auditing enabled, choosing JSON when Splunk parsers expect structured paths and text only for legacy syslog bridges. Set log level high enough to capture dashboard writes without drowning in datasource health noise; trim noisy categories through allowlists if vendor documentation supports it. For Grafana Enterprise, configure audit sinks per vendor guidance: file tailing through Fluent Bit or Promtail to HTTP Event Collector, direct HTTP shipping to a Loki-compatible endpoint that forwards to Splunk, or webhooks that POST normalized events to HEC. Open-source deployments rely on file logs, sidecar collectors, or community audit forwarders; normalize sourcetype=grafana:audit with props.conf LINE_BREAKER, TIME_FORMAT, and FIELDALIAS maps for dashboard_uid, user, org_id, and action.

Enterprise webhook lane: register HTTPS endpoints backed by Splunk HEC tokens scoped to the observability index. Verify TLS, rotate tokens quarterly, and reject cleartext replay. Map dashboard saved and version restored style events into sourcetype=grafana:webhook with explicit fields for uid and version integers when available.

GitOps lanes: ensure Argo CD emits application controller audit lines including sync operations that touch dashboard ConfigMaps or Grafana provisioning paths. For Flux, ship kustomize-controller or helm-controller logs and reconciliation events that reference dashboard providers or sidecar reload annotations. If you use a dashboard reloader sidecar, include its log stream so mechanical reloads are distinguishable from human saves.

CIM alignment: accelerate the Change data model for objects tagged with object_category=dashboard. Use props and transforms on grafana:audit and grafana:webhook to populate Change.action, Change.user, Change.object, and Change.object_category so tstats arms remain truthful. Document which extractions are TA-owned versus local.

Time synchronization: every collector host must use authenticated NTP. Record forwarder time zone assumptions explicitly so after-hours logic in SPL matches human change windows.

Secret hygiene: HEC tokens live in password storage on forwarders; Grafana webhook shared secrets rotate with the same ticket as Grafana admin password changes. Never paste bearer tokens into this repository.

Operational proof after configuration: run a lab save and confirm three independent paths see the event within one minute: raw grafana:audit row, optional grafana:webhook delivery, and a CIM Change row if acceleration is on. Fail the change request if any path is silent.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as grafana_uc_13_6_7_dashboard_version_lineage with dispatch earliest=-30d@h and latest=now on a fifteen-minute schedule for production, moving to hourly if indexer load requires it. Alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH after deduplication by dashboard_uid and user.

multisearch rationale: arm one reads grafana:audit with match filters on action text and raw payload hints so save, restore, version, revert, and rollback verbs surface even when field names shift. Arm two ingests grafana:webhook for enterprise-delivered dashboard events that never touch the file audit path. Arm three queries the accelerated Change model with Change.action in modified or created and Change.object_category=dashboard so compliance reviewers can reuse the same narrative as other change analytics. Arm four pulls argocd:audit and flux:reconcile lines that imply GitOps touched dashboard providers, separating automation cadence from interactive edits.

coalesce and outer evals unify dashboard_uid, user, and org_id across heterogeneous schemas so downstream stats do not fragment on nulls. The sort and streamstats pair measures seconds since the previous edit for the same uid, exposing bursty edit wars. streamstats with a one-hour time window counts saves per user for HIGH severity when a single principal exceeds five actions in an hour. eventstats counts total rows per dashboard_uid across the thirty-day window to approximate baseline edit frequency and populate baseline_edits_per_day.

join with inputlookup grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv enriches classification, executive flags, GitOps ownership, and scheduled_window text. case maps CRITICAL when a revert-class action hits an executive dashboard outside a described change window, HIGH for burst saves, MEDIUM for after-hours modifications, and LOW for in-window routine churn.

Throttle alerts: suppress duplicate CRITICAL rows for the same dashboard_uid for sixty minutes unless version number jumps again. Include recommended_owner from the lookup in the alert payload for routing.

Fenced SPL in this document must match the spl JSON field exactly; CI treats divergence as failure.

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.7 Grafana dashboard version lineage: fuse audit, enterprise webhook, CIM Change dashboards, GitOps reconcile. Baseline window 30d; tune saves_per_hour thresholds; no draft tokens in production.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="grafana:audit" earliest=-30d@h latest=now
      (dashboard OR dashboard_uid OR uid OR "dashboard_uid")
      | eval act_lc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action, verb, operation, request_action, ""))))
      | eval raw_lc=lower(_raw)
      | where match(act_lc,"(save|saved|update|restore|restored|version|revert|rollback)") OR match(raw_lc,"(save|restore|version|revert|rollback).*dashboard|dashboard.*(save|restore|version|revert|rollback)")
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, uid, dashboardUid, dashboardUID, resource_uid, ""))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user, username, login, actor, user_email, "")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id, orgId, orgID, organization_id, "")))
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action, verb, operation, "audit_action"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_audit" ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="grafana:webhook" earliest=-30d@h latest=now
      (dashboard OR "dashboard-version" OR uid)
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, uid, dashboardUid, dashboardUID, ""))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user, username, editor, triggeredBy, "")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id, orgId, orgID, "")))
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(event_type, action, hookType, "webhook_event"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_webhook" ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t latest(_time) AS _time FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-30d@h latest=now (Change.action="modified" OR Change.action="created") Change.object_category="dashboard" BY Change.object Change.user Change.action
      | rename Change.object AS dashboard_uid Change.user AS user Change.action AS cim_action
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(dashboard_uid)))
      | eval user=trim(toString(user))
      | eval org_id="unknown"
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(cim_action)))
      | eval lane="cim_change" ]
    [ search index=cicd (sourcetype="argocd:audit" OR sourcetype="flux:reconcile") earliest=-30d@h latest=now
      (grafana OR dashboard OR ConfigMap OR configmap OR provider)
      | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, uid, ""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?i)(?:uid|dashboard)[=:\"\s]+(?<rex_uid>[A-Za-z0-9_-]{6,64})"
      | eval dashboard_uid=if(len(dashboard_uid)>0, dashboard_uid, lower(trim(toString(mvindex(rex_uid,0)))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user, username, principal, triggeredBy, "gitops_controller")))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id, cluster, "")))
      | eval event_action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(verb, operation, reason, "gitops_reconcile"))))
      | eval lane="gitops" ]
| eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid, ""))))
| eval user=coalesce(nullif(trim(user),""), "unknown_actor")
| eval org_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(org_id),""), "unknown_org")
| where len(dashboard_uid) > 3
| eval action_normalized=lower(trim(toString(event_action)))
| sort 0 dashboard_uid + _time
| streamstats window=1 current=f global=f last(_time) AS prev_ts BY dashboard_uid
| eval secs_since_last_edit=if(isnotnull(prev_ts) AND prev_ts>0, round(_time - prev_ts, 0), null())
| streamstats time_window=3600s global=f count AS saves_last_hour BY user
| eventstats count AS edits_baseline_30d BY dashboard_uid
| eval baseline_edits_per_day=round(edits_baseline_30d / 30, 3)
| eval hr=tonumber(strftime(_time, "%H"), 10)
| eval after_hours=if((hr < 7 OR hr > 18), 1, 0)
| eval is_revert=if(match(action_normalized, "(revert|rollback|restore)"), 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 dashboard_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv
     | fields dashboard_uid, title, folder, owner, classification, is_executive, gitops_provider, scheduled_window
     | eval dashboard_uid=lower(trim(toString(dashboard_uid))) ]
| eval is_exec=if(match(lower(toString(is_executive)), "^(1|true|yes)$"), 1, 0)
| eval sched_lc=lower(trim(toString(scheduled_window)))
| eval in_change_window=if(match(sched_lc, "(cab|approved|maintenance|green|saturday|sunday|sat|sun)"), 1, if(len(sched_lc)==0, 0, 1))
| eval severity=case(
    is_revert==1 AND is_exec==1 AND in_change_window==0, "CRITICAL",
    saves_last_hour > 5, "HIGH",
    after_hours==1 AND match(action_normalized, "(save|update|modified|created)"), "MEDIUM",
    true(), "LOW"
  )
| table _time, dashboard_uid, title, folder, user, org_id, lane, action_normalized, saves_last_hour, secs_since_last_edit, baseline_edits_per_day, severity, classification, is_executive, gitops_provider

```

savedsearches.conf sketch: cron every fifteen minutes, dispatch earliest=-30d@h, alert when severity in CRITICAL HIGH, email or webhook to observability governance queue with fields dashboard_uid, title, user, severity, lane.

Step 4 — Validate

Validation begins in a non-production org. Use curl against /api/dashboards/db with a service token to POST a trivial panel change, then call /api/dashboards/uid/:uid/versions to read version integers. Confirm a matching grafana:audit row, webhook if enabled, and CIM Change extracted row within the sampling interval.

Trigger a restore or revert through the Grafana UI on a disposable dashboard and verify the audit action includes from_version or equivalent metadata. If your deployment omits from_version, open a vendor ticket but keep alerting on action text.

GitOps validation: merge a dashboard JSON change through your canonical repository, wait for Argo CD or Flux sync, and confirm index=cicd shows sourcetype=argocd:audit or flux:reconcile with strings referencing the dashboard uid or ConfigMap name. Ensure the multisearch gitops arm increments counts without manual save noise.

Lookup validation: load grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv into the search app and run inputlookup alone to confirm dashboard_uid keys match live Grafana exports. Spot-check an executive dashboard row with is_executive true and scheduled_window populated.

Negative control: after validation, leave the dashboard idle for two hours and confirm the alert search returns no CRITICAL or HIGH rows when filtering with where severity in CRITICAL HIGH.

Synthetic after-hours test: schedule a benign save during a maintenance window listed in scheduled_window and confirm severity stays LOW or MEDIUM per policy. Repeat outside the window with the same test account and confirm MEDIUM or CRITICAL escalation paths fire in staging only.

Performance: if Job Inspector shows tstats dominating cost, narrow the Change arm to summariesonly=true with a five-minute span or materialize nightly version-event summaries keyed on dashboard_uid.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Audit feature disabled in grafana.ini: Grafana restarts after upgrades silently flip auditing flags; compare live config maps or ini files to source control and re-enable auditing with a tracked change ticket.

Case B — user or org_id missing in forwarded events: FIELDALIAS maps lag behind vendor JSON; update props.conf, rebuild knowledge bundle, and replay a day of raw data in a sandbox index to validate extractions.

Case C — Webhook delivery returns HTTP 401: HEC token mismatch or wrong Authorization scheme; rotate token, update Grafana webhook secret, and tail splunkd access.log for the forwarder.

Case D — CIM Change rows stay zero: data model acceleration is off or nodename filters exclude dashboard objects; enable acceleration, verify tags on eventtypes, and run a simple datamodel search before returning to tstats.

Case E — Lookup grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv empty: curator export job failed; rerun Grafana API inventory script, confirm CSV landed in lookups folder, and rebuild periodic scheduled input if used.

Case F — GitOps reconcile loop drowns saves: Flux or Argo repeatedly patches the same ConfigMap; inspect diff errors, pause automation, and fix kustomize ordering before muting Splunk detections.

Case G — Clock skew across HA Grafana nodes: audit timestamps alternate apparent order; fix NTP, then backfill severity logic with host-specific offsets only as a temporary mitigation.

Case H — Cosmetic auto-saves spike volume: panel resize storms increment dashboardVersion without semantic changes; correlate with browser session telemetry and raise thresholds for LOW noise.

Case I — Revert events lack from_version: rely on action text and version API correlation; enrich with a subsearch against versions endpoint only for investigations to avoid API rate limits.

Case J — Provisioning overrides silently overwrite UI saves: dashboard provider refresh_order and disableDeletion flags mismatch expectations; align GitOps repo with interactive edits via automated pull requests.

Case K — JSON versus text audit formats break extractions: dual sourcetypes or inconsistent KV_MODE; split props stanzas and add SEDCMD to strip prefixes before JSON parsing.

Escalation: CRITICAL rows on executive dashboards route to the named owner in the lookup plus the Head of Platform deputy within fifteen minutes. HIGH rows page the observability on-call with a requirement to open a governance ticket. MEDIUM rows open a business-hours task for dashboard guild review.

Weekly governance: review a sample of LOW severity bursts to catch automation mistakes early. Archive weekly CSV exports of alert results to a restricted evidence index for audit readiness.

Closing checklist: multisearch lists audit, webhook, CIM Change, and GitOps arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, and case appear; join uses grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv; final table exposes fifteen analyst columns including lane and baseline metrics.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a careful diary of how the team’s wall charts and dial boards change over time, who nudged them, and when someone pressed undo on a big picture. If a chart suddenly confuses leaders during a stressful week, we can roll back to the version people trusted and show exactly what flipped.
hardware: Grafana OSS, Grafana Enterprise, or Grafana Cloud stacks that expose audit logs, webhooks, or API-accessible dashboard version metadata.
